frameworks.name: signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: priming shifts sensitivity, serial dependence shifts criterion as orthogonal dimensions

frameworks.name: visual priming (priming of pop-out)
frameworks.one_line: facilitation from repeated stimulus features measured concurrently with serial dependence

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: systematic bias of current report toward prior stimuli, here orientation

frameworks.name: attention
frameworks.one_line: proposed mechanism by which priming modulates perceptual sensitivity

core_question: Do visual priming and serial dependence share a common perceptual mechanism, or are they mediated by separate processes?
key_assumptions: reaction time and accuracy index sensitivity changes
orientation reproduction biases index criterion shifts
priming and serial dependence can be dissociated within a single paradigm
signal detection theory's sensitivity/criterion distinction applies
manipulations: repetition vs. change of singleton color across trials
repetition vs. change of singleton position across trials
key_findings: strong priming effects on reaction time and accuracy accumulated over runs
serial dependence on orientation was strong but did not depend on previous color or run length
reaction times correlated with orientation reproduction imprecision
reaction times were uncorrelated with magnitude of serial dependence
interpretations: priming and serial dependence are mediated by different neural mechanisms
priming affects sensitivity (attention-like), serial dependence affects criteria — orthogonal SDT dimensions
